feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Try with ./Sapup unlocksys
S4 Assign Contact Person to a BP multiple time with different function/dept
entry.content:
Hello,

I need some help regarding BP contact persons in S4 Hana. The migration process from ECC has resulted in a new BP contact person being created for each customer contact person in ECC. In some cases we have the same person created multiple times because of the different department and functions they were assigned in ECC. 

We would like to stop this practice and just create one BP contact person for John Smith, say number 123. 

A BUR001 relationship would then be created making 123 a contact person for an organisation, 4444. The department and function assigned would make it the delivery contact. 

Another BUR001 relationship would then be created making 123 a contact person for 4444. The department and function assigned would make it the pricing contact.

Another BUR001 relationship for the same objects, but the dept and function make it the quality contact. 

It is possible to have one contact person assigned to multiple accounts. But not to have one contact person assigned multiple times to one account. 

When you try this S4 gives the error "Relationship already exists; further interval not possible"

Any suggestions on how to solve this issue? I cannot see a standard method. 

Thanks 

Phil

entry.title: OP_API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002 not able to pack or unpack HU items (Existing HUs)
entry.content:
During our implementation of OP_API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002 we have not found a way to create HU Items for existing Handling units via the delivery API. API Documentation only gives an example of simultaneous HU header & HU item creation via one batch request here, however our use case changing HU content after initial creation is not met.

Looking into using the Handling Unit API though it would be ideal to have this functionality included in the delivery API.

A post request on just HU items without HU header returns error "No header found for the HandlingUnitExternalId". 

 

 

 

{
   "error":{
      "code":"LOHUM_DEL_API/002",
      "message":{
         "lang":"en",
         "value":"No header found for the HandlingUnitExternalId 00000000000300000208"
      }
   }  
}

 

 

 

A batch request that includes PUT or PATCH on the HU header+ POST request(s) for new HU item returns error "HTTP method 'PUT' is not supported by this entityset (HU Header)". 

 

 

 

 

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

entry.title: Upgrade from R/3 to S/4 - Payroll and Time advices
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

did you upgraded you R/3 payroll and time solution ?

Any advice how to do it smoothly ?

What about an existing integration between R/3 and SF to be upgrade to S/4 ?

Thanks in advance.

Kind Regards,

Lucien.

entry.title: ALTER USER Statement on HANA DB
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Based on the SAP Note: 1702224 - Disable password lifetime for technical users,
I am considering running the following statement:

ALTER USER SYSTEM DISABLE PASSWORD LIFETIME.

Can anyone help me with the questions below?
- Does this take effect immediately without restarting the system?
- Are there any prerequisites or concerns before running the command on the production system?

entry.title: SAP BAS, sap/hana-client and SAP HANA on-prem: “Cannot resolve host name”
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

We are trying to access our SAP HANA on-premise database within SAP BAS, but we are already having problems logging in because the host cannot be resolved:

 

 

Error: Connection failed (RTE:[89001] Cannot resolve host name 'hana.on_prem.local' rc=-2: Name or service not known (hana.on_prem.local))

 

 

 

Here is the small mini script (nodejs sap/hana-client):

If we run this code locally in VS Code while we are in the company network, the host can be resolved and we can successfully connect to our SAP HANA on-premise, so we suspect the error is in the BTP or CloudConnector.


The associated Cloud Connector in the BTP subaccount is properly set up and "reachable":

 

 


 

 Do we need to specify anything else in the parameters for the “conn.connect…” call?
Perhaps location_id or something similar?

Is our assumption correct that “conn.connect…” uses a TCP connection?

Do we then need SOCKS5? If so, how do we set this up?

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/using-tcp-protocol-for-cloud-applications?q=socks5

We are grateful for any advice!

Many thanks and best regards,

Marco

entry.title: Not able to login to ABAP ADT
entry.content:
Hi team,

I am not able to login to my BTP trail account. Everytime I click on eclipse "Copy to Clipboard" buttona adn open link in browser either in incog or normal. its asking for id and password and upon providing the credentials, again asking for same creds.

note: my btp trail account has almost 50 days left out of 90 days free trial, tried incog window as well.

Requesting your help on this

entry.title: Refresh Option in reports
entry.content:
Dear all, 

In some reports there is an option of refreshing report.

Need to add same option to FMRP_RW_BUDCON, please suggest how to make it

entry.title: Equal Workload distribution to HANA Scale out database servers.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There are 8 databases in HANA 2.0 SP05 Rev 59 DB scale out environment.

I noticed a SQL query is running on the particular db server and spike up CPU /Memory more while other DB hosts are less utilized.

* How to equalize that SQL execution to all DB hosts. Please provide more details if there is a solution.

* if one DB host is spiked up, will it hang the whole DB response to application server?

Please answer.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Which certification is required to install an SAP HANA Tailored Data Center Integration (TDI) system
entry.content:
Which certification is required to install an SAP HANA Tailored Data Center Integration (TDI) system?

SAP HANA Tailored Data Center Integration (TDI) Overview says:

Let only certified person(s) do the SAP HANA software installation

  • Required exam: C_HANATEC_16 (*) - See SAP Training and Certification Shop for details
  • The exam needs to be successfully passed for a person to perform SAP HANA SW installations
    • SAP HANA hardware partners and their employees do not need this certificate
    • Companies or their employees, who are sub-contractors of hardware partners must be certified to perform HANA SW installations

The highest versions of the exam I can find reference to are:

  • C_HANATEC_17 (Get Certified: C_HANATEC_17 - SAP Community)
  • C_HANATEC_19 (C_HANATEC_19 |SAP Certified Technology 2.0 SPS07 Exam | Udemy)

Searching for 'HANATEC' in Learning Journeys - Overview gives no results.

entry.title: SAP HANA Native Storage Extension
entry.content:
Our production database is currently using an estimate of 64% of used memory in the database and we are trying to implement SAP NSE for the first time to reduce its utilization.

 

We would like to ask the following items before implementing it to production

  1. What will be the impact in performance as the database table's growth increases over time if you apply NSE?
  2. As the table increase, what is also the effect of those tables/partitions that were sent to the buffer (pageloadable) when a user tries to access the table and move it back to the memory?
  3. How can we verify the changes/trend in of each metric (memory used, allocated, DB size, etc.)

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Issue with Transportation Route Proposal in Export Documents
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently facing the following situation:

At present, when the consignee and the ultimate consignee are located in different countries, the transportation route in an export document only reflects the departure country and the country of the ultimate consignee. The consignee’s country is not taken into account.
This means the route currently appears as: (Departure Country - Ultimate Consignee's Country).

However, if the consignee and ultimate consignee are in different countries, the route should instead be displayed as:
(Departure Country - Consignee's Country - Ultimate Consignee's Country).

We have already looked into the BAdI /SAPSLL/BADI_CDOC_PROP_N to see if this could be achieved by manipulating the header data in the data proposal logic. Unfortunately, this does not seem to be possible for the route topic.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or found an alternative approach to solving this?

Thanks in advance for any insights!

Best wishes

entry.title: How to add more than 1 email notification recipient in a job in SM37
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to add multiple email recipient inside the email notification section in a job in SM37. 

Is there a way to add more than 1 email recipient in the same job? Or there's a limitation for only 1 recipient?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Brownfield Migration: Successful transition to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The transition to SAP S/4HANA is a crucial step in the digital transformation of companies. One possible migration strategy is brownfield migration , which involves converting the existing SAP ERP system to the new platform without redesigning processes and data from scratch.

But what challenges does a brownfield migration entail? A concrete example is the change in the length of material numbers , which requires companies to make technical and organizational adjustments.

What is an SAP Brownfield Migration?

SAP Brownfield Migration is a technical conversion process in which an existing SAP ECC system is migrated to SAP S/4HANA. Unlike Greenfield Migration , which involves building a new system, Brownfield Migration preserves existing processes, data, and custom code .

This strategy is suitable for companies that want to preserve their existing investments in the ERP system while benefiting from the advantages of SAP S/4HANA.

Challenges of Brownfield Migration: Example Material Number Length

One of the technical changes in SAP S/4HANA concerns the length of material numbers . In SAP ECC, material numbers were limited to 18 characters . With S/4HANA, this can be extended to 40 characters .

This change seems simple at first glance, but can have far-reaching consequences:

  1. Impact on custom code and in-house developments

Many companies have customized programs and reports that work with the existing material number length. Adjustments are necessary if:

  • In-house developments that have stored the material number as a fixed character length
  • ABAP programs work with manual character length restrictions
  • Reports and ALV lists are aligned to the old material number structure
  1. Adjustments in databases and interfaces

The extension of the material number can affect external systems, including:

  • Interfaces to suppliers or partners (EDI, B2B portals)
  • Merchandise management and warehouse management systems linked to SAP
  • Databases in which the material number is stored as a key
  1. Impact on printed forms and processes

Material numbers appear in many business processes, e.g.:

  • Invoices, orders and delivery notes
  • Product labels and packaging information
  • Workflows in which material numbers are used as reference values

Best practices for a successful brownfield migration

To ensure a smooth SAP Brownfield migration, companies should follow a structured approach :

  1. Analysis of the existing system landscape

Before migration, it's important to check which adjustments to custom code, data, and processes are necessary. SAP offers the Readiness Check , which identifies potential problems early on.

  1. Custom Code Adaptation

Existing ABAP code must be analyzed and adapted to be compatible with the new requirements of S/4HANA. This particularly applies to:

  • Database accesses with fixed field lengths
  • Selection masks and reports with material numbers
  • Programs that use material numbers for logic or comparison operations
  1. Data migration and cleansing
  • Checking whether material numbers in historical data are consistent
  • Cleanup and adaptation of old data structures
  • Ensure that external systems support the new material number length
  1. Testing and quality assurance
  • Conducting end-to-end tests to detect errors early
  • Training for users to ensure that the new processes are understood
  • Post-migration monitoring to quickly resolve performance and compatibility issues

Conclusion: Brownfield migration as a strategic decision

SAP Brownfield Migration is an effective way to transition to SAP S/4HANA with lower risk and lower cost than a Greenfield implementation. However, technical challenges such as material number length require detailed planning and careful adaptation of custom code and interfaces .

Companies should consider the impact of the migration early on and develop a structured migration and testing plan to ensure a smooth transformation.

Further information

Would you like to learn more about brownfield migration or receive customized consulting for your company? Contact us for a comprehensive analysis of your SAP system landscape.

entry.title: Unexpected Daily Backup in HANA DB – Where Else Could It Be Scheduled?
entry.content:
Hello,

A previous consultant scheduled a backup in our HANA DB, but I can't find where it's coming from. I checked DB13 and backint, but there's no scheduled backup there. There's also no dedicated backup user created in HANA DB.

I found a data backup schedule in HANA Cockpit and canceled it, yet backups are still being taken under the name "Daily Backup." Even after disabling HANA Cockpit scheduling, the backup continues. I suspected XS Engine, but in the HANA DB configuration, the scheduler under xsengine is set to "false." I also checked the crontabs on Linux, and they are empty.

Where else could this data backup be scheduled? Any help would be appreciated!

entry.title: what is the maximum number of lines we can create for a contract?
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

kindly help in information related to the maximum number of lines allowed to create a contract in SAP HANA?
Client is having 18,000-line items for a contract in their Legacy System. How to manage this situation kindly help me.

Thanks,

Divya

entry.title: Unable to Clear Multiple Documents at Once in FB05
entry.content:
I’m facing an issue when clearing multiple documents at once using FB05.

When I select and clear each document pair individually, the clearing process works correctly. However, when I select multiple documents together, the system does not clear the entire amount. Instead, only a portion of the selected documents get cleared, leaving an unexpected balance.

I checked OB74 (Automatic Clearing Rules) to ensure relevant fields (e.g., ZUONR, GSBER, VBOUND, VERTN, VBEWA, UMSKZ) are consistent across documents. And no locked documents.

If there are any system restrictions preventing multiple documents from being cleared at once in FB05?

Pls help me.

entry.title: Priority for source of supply determination
entry.content:
I want to know if possible to configure a rule for prioritizing the source of supply of a purchase requisition. 

1) I have 2 vendors which have source lists, info record but the system proposes both of them in the source of supply. We run MRP and the systema assignes one info record, what is the logic behind the selection?



2) I want when running MRP, there is one being selected over the other one without having to "fix" the vendor in the source list.. is this possible? if I enter a "1" in field MRP in source list, how would the system determine the source of supply then? 

 

Vendor 1 has 16 days delivery time, price for material is 187.68 EUR

Vendor 2 has 10 days planned delivery time and price 188.36 EUR

None of them have and agreement in place.

entry.title: Implementation of HANA SSLENFORCEMENT
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

 

We are implementing the PERSON_NAME SSL ENFORCEMNT in SHC PERSON_NAME DB, we have gone through the below sap notes and we could see some points are missing in the sap notes, so could you please provide the required details of this activity.

 

 

 

2475246 - How to configure HANA DB connections using SSL from ABAP instance
 
2475246 - How to configure HANA DB connections using SSL from ABAP instance - SAP for Me
 
3168368 - After setting sslenforce as true in HANA database, the R3trans, transp
 
3168368 - After setting sslenforce as true in HANA database, the R3trans, transport in ABAP are failing - SAP for Me
 
 2475246 - How to configure HANA DB connections using SSL from ABAP instance
 1761693 - Other connection options for SAP HANA
 1827566 - How to set environment variables for SAP system
 3158257 - FAQ: SAP HANA User Store (hdbuserstore)
 2040644 - Secure Internal Server Communication
 
2795610 - Enabling SSO in HANA Cockpit Manager of SAP HANA Cockpit 2 Fails When sslEnforce=true on the Remote SAP HANA System


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: The accumulate value of HANA graphical calculation view.
entry.content:
Hi experts,

How to get the accumulate value with the graphical calculation view of SAP HANA, not SQL script view ?

For example :

MON   AMOUNT   accumulate AMOUNT

01         100           100

02         200           300

03         300           600

...
